revision.text:
'''西小山古墳'''（にしこやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]] [[泉南郡]][[岬町]]にある[[古墳時代]]中期の[[円墳]]である。[[西陵古墳]]などが所在する、淡輪古墳群の内の１つである
== 概要==
[[西陵古墳]]と[[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]]に近接する、径４０ｍの円墳である。開墾が行なわれることになり、昭和５年（１９３０年）に当時、[[京都大学]]考古学研究室にいた[[末永雅雄]]が担当して、[[発掘調査]]が実施された。墳頂部からは、長さ３，３ｍ、幅０，７ｍの竪穴石室が検出された。石室の南北両側壁から、鉄刀２３点、鋒２点、鉄鏃１０７点が出土し、石室東端から三角板鋲留短甲、金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑1点が出土、石室西端から鋲留式頸甲1点、肩甲１点、圭甲小札約８００枚が出土した。金銅装眉庇付冑の場合の、他の出例としては、仁徳陵前方部出土例のほか、大阪府[[北天平塚古墳]]、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]] 三重県佐久米古墳 福岡県、月ノ岡古墳、千葉県祇園山古墳などが知られる。西小山古墳の築造時期は出土遺物から、５世紀後葉ごろと考えられる。この調査で出土した頸甲、肩甲、圭甲小札の復原品を末永雅雄が製作し、構造、製作技法の研究につとめたことが知られている。
==参考文献 == 『末永雅雄先生 生誕百周年記念 古墳研究の歩み』 大阪狭山市立郷土資料館 １９９７年 ２２項 [[category:円墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:泉南郡]]

'''西小山古墳'''（にしこやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]] [[泉南郡]][[岬町]]にある[[古墳時代]]中期の[[円墳]]である。[[西陵古墳]]などが所在する、淡輪古墳群の内の１つである。
== 概要== [[西陵古墳]]と[[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]]に近接する、径40メートルの円墳である。開墾が行なわれることになり、昭和5年（1930年）に当時、[[京都大学]]考古学研究室にいた[[末永雅雄]]が担当して、[[発掘調査]]が実施された。墳頂部からは、長さ3.3メートル、幅0.7メートルの竪穴石室が検出された。石室の南北両側壁から、鉄刀23点、鋒2点、鉄鏃107点が出土し、石室東端から三角板鋲留短甲、金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑1点が出土、石室西端から鋲留式頸甲1点、肩甲1点、圭甲小札約800枚が出土した。金銅装眉庇付冑の場合の、他の出例としては、仁徳陵前方部出土例のほか、大阪府[[北天平塚古墳]]、奈良県[[五条猫塚古墳]] 三重県佐久米古墳 福岡県、月ノ岡古墳、千葉県祇園山古墳などが知られる。西小山古墳の築造時期は出土遺物から、5世紀後葉ごろと考えられる。この調査で出土した頸甲、肩甲、圭甲小札の復原品を末永雅雄が製作し、構造、製作技法の研究につとめたことが知られている。
==参考文献 == 『末永雅雄先生 生誕百周年記念 古墳研究の歩み』大阪狭山市立郷土資料館 1997年 22項 [[category:円墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:泉南郡]]

'''西小山古墳'''（にしこやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]]にある[[古墳時代]]中期の[[円墳]]である。[[西陵古墳]]などが所在する、淡輪古墳群の内の1つである。

１９８１年の大阪府教育委員会の調査結果による。但し、造り出し有としているが、これは帆立貝型古墳である。

| image =

| coordinates = 
| type = Circular Kofun (round mound)
| map_size = Diameter 40 meters
| burial = Vertical stone chamber

| burial goods = Gold-copper-plated {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}, [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]], [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]]
| Historic Site  = None
| notes = 
}}

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]].

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|qq=|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|title=Centennial Memorial of Masao Suenaga: The Progress of Kofun Research|publisher=Osaka Sayama Municipal Local Museum|year=1997|pages=22}}
* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}

The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]].

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

| Historic Site  = None
| notes = 
}}

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]].

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|title=Centennial Memorial of Masao Suenaga: The Progress of Kofun Research|publisher=Osaka Sayama Municipal Local Museum|year=1997|pages=22}}
* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}

| Historic Site  = None
| notes =

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]].

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|title=Centennial Memorial of Masao Suenaga: The Progress of Kofun Research|publisher=Osaka Sayama Municipal Local Museum|year=1997|pages=22}}
* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}

| Historic Site  = None

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]].

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|title=Centennial Memorial of Masao Suenaga: The Progress of Kofun Research|publisher=Osaka Sayama Municipal Local Museum|year=1997|pages=22}}
* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}

| burial goods = Gold-copper-plated {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}, [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]], [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]]
| Historic Site  = None
| notes = 
}}

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]].

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|title=Centennial Memorial of Masao Suenaga: The Progress of Kofun Research|publisher=Osaka Sayama Municipal Local Museum|year=1997|pages=22}}
* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Nishiryo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 [[:en:Chokutō|Iron swords]]
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]]
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Nishiryo Kofun}}.

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Nishiryo Kofun}} and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 {{ill|Chokutō|en|Chokutō|WD=Q63821|lt=Iron swords}}
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted {{ill|Tankō|en|Tankō|WD=Q1687003|lt=Tankō}}
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}

Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

'''Nishikoyama Kofun''' (にしこやまこふん) is a mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} Kofun located in Tannowa, Misaki Town, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also includes the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Nishiryo Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}}.

== Overview ==
The Nishikoyama Kofun is a scallop-shaped Kofun with a diameter of 50 meters, located near {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Nishiryo Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}} and {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}. The mound was excavated in 1930 under the direction of {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} from the Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. A vertical stone chamber measuring 3.3 meters in length and 0.7 meters in width was discovered at the mound's summit. burial goods recovered from the stone chamber included:
- 23 {{ill|Chokutō|en|Chokutō|WD=Q63821|lt=Iron swords|1=Chokutō}}
- 2 spears
- 107 iron arrowheads
- Triangular plate-riveted {{ill|Tankō|en|Tankō|WD=Q1687003|lt=Tankō|1=Tankō}}
- A gold-copper-plated riveted {{ill|Helmet with Brow Plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}
- A riveted neck guard and shoulder guards
- Approximately 800 lamellar plates for {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}}

This type of gold-copper-plated helmet with a brow plate has also been found in other notable sites such as the {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|仁徳陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, Kitatenhirazuka Kofun in Osaka, {{ill|Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|ja|五条猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|1=Gojō Nekozuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372490}} in Nara, Sakume Kofun in Mie, {{ill|Tsukioka Kofun|ja|月岡古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka, and Gionyama Kofun in Chiba. Based on the burial goods, the mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century.{{Sfn|Suenaga|1934}}

== Mound Investigation ==
In 1981, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation of the mound. The vertical stone chamber had already been flattened and lost, but remnants of paving stones and a haniwa (clay figurine) arrangement were well-preserved. The original diameter of the mound is estimated to have been 50 meters, with a height of 7 meters, and it was constructed in two tiers. A protruding area (造り出し) was identified on the northwest side. Traces of a surrounding moat were inferred from the paddy field ridges in the vicinity. The excavated haniwa included cylindrical, bell-shaped, and lid-shaped types, with 15% of them made from high-hardness Sue pottery. The paving stones were made from Izumi sandstone, sourced locally.{{Sfn|Kuze|1968}}

== Historic Site ==
=== Important designation1 (National designation1) ===
- Gold-copper-plated helmet with brow plate from Nishikoyama Kofun, Izumi Province (archaeological material). Designated on August 26, 1938.{{Sfn|designation1 Database|201}}{{Sfn|Osaka Prefectural Website}}
** Note: This artifact was stolen in 1955.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Press Release, 2017}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|title=Centennial Memorial of Masao Suenaga: The Progress of Kofun Research|publisher=Osaka Sayama Municipal Local Museum|year=1997|pages=22}}
* {{Cite book|title=Tannowa Site Excavation Summary III|year=1981|pages=56-67}}
* {{Cite book|author=Hitoshi Kuze|title=The Story of Izumi Archaeological Sites|year=2004|pages=7-10}}
